failure_surface: Output contains static numeric values instead of Excel-formatted formulas; evaluation test cases failed because cells lack required Excel formula references that would dynamically calculate based on source data.
root_cause: The agent misinterpreted 'write formulas in Excel' as a request to perform calculations in Python and write resulting values to cells, rather than writing actual Excel formula strings (e.g., '=A1+B1') to cells so Excel would compute them natively.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When instructions explicitly mention 'formulas', assign Excel formula strings to cells via openpyxl (value starts with '=') rather than pre-calculating in Python and writing static numbers.
evidence: Instruction explicitly states: 'How can I write formulas in Excel to populate...' indicating the desired output should contain Excel formulas, not static values
Final solution writes: ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=2).value = 1 + time_to_serial(block1[0]) which computes in Python and writes a static float
No Excel formula string (starting with '=') was assigned to any cell in the final code
The instruction mentions 'return...times as numbers' which can be satisfied by either approach, but the 'formulas' keyword determines the correct method
Test case evaluation returned 0, indicating output format mismatch with expected formula-based cells

memory_items.title: Formula Intent Detection
memory_items.lesson: When instructions mention writing formulas in spreadsheets, write Excel-formatted formula strings to cells, not pre-calculated Python values.
memory_items.generalization: Always check if task requires Excel-native formulas (=references) versus static data transformation; formulas must begin with '=' when written via openpyxl.

memory_items.title: Distinguish Calculation Location
memory_items.lesson: Python-side computation produces static values; Excel-side computation requires writing formula strings to preserve dynamic recalculation capability.
memory_items.generalization: For tasks requesting formulas, convert logic to Excel formula notation and assign formula strings to cells rather than computing in Python.

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL Formula Syntax
memory_items.lesson: To write a formula to a cell using openpyxl, set cell.value to a string beginning with '=' using standard Excel reference syntax.
memory_items.generalization: Formula assignment pattern: cell.value = '=SOME_FUNCTION(REFERENCE)' ensures Excel evaluates it, whereas cell.value = <number> creates static content.

edits.content: **Formula Intent:** When task instructions explicitly request 'formulas', write Excel formula strings (starting with '=') to cells via openpyxl, not pre-computed values from Python. Static values lose dynamic recalculation capability. Always distinguish between transforming data values versus inserting Excel-formula logic that recalculates when source data changes.
